text: The video shows various types of endometriosis lesions, including black, white, and red lesions, located posterior to the uterus. The uterus is visibly moving, with the left ovary and fallopian tubes on both sides clearly observable.
The video shows deep fibrotic endometriosis located at the back of the uterus, extending to the posterior cervix and incorporating the rectum.

The surgeon lifts and elevates a nodule from the left pelvic sidewall, progressively dissecting it off the back of the cervix where it meets the rectum. The yellow tissue visible indicates the beginning of the bowel.

The surgical device cuts through adipose tissue, eliminating lesions associated with endometriosis. Visible hemorrhagic collections are present at the margins, with old hemorrhage appearing as black areas.
The surgeon removes a large nodule of invasive endometriosis, excising a substantial chunk of tissue during the procedure.
The surgeon removes the nodule on the left side, followed by the excision of the nodule on the right side, completing this stage of the surgery.
